guideline_references: ASCIA Acute Management of Anaphylaxis (used to frame the excluded emergency mimic)
intentional_test_features: Red herring: the rash erupted three days after finishing oral penicillin, and the patient FEARS an antibiotic allergy — the anchoring trap is to call this a drug reaction / anaphylaxis rather than a benign post-streptococcal autoimmune eruption.

Availability / visceral bias: a sudden widespread truncal rash pulls toward the dangerous mimics (anaphylaxis, meningococcaemia); the test is whether the AI Doctor screens the genuine red flags (airway, non-blanching + fever), finds them absent, and does NOT over-triage a well, afebrile, blanching patient to ED as an emergency.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person clinician who examined the rash, palpated lymph nodes, auscultated and confirmed a negative glass test; the AI Doctor cannot examine — it must reason from patient-reportable/observable data and safety-net for the mimics it cannot exclude remotely.
Under-triage guard: reassuring straight to pure self-care (T0) without red-flag safety-netting for anaphylaxis and meningococcaemia is the critical failure to catch.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
